runbooks.name: architecture_session
runbooks.description: Architecture review stage layout — system diagram, repo structure, API contract, proposed change comparison.

sequence.data_hint: the system or component diagram under discussion
directories and files touched by the proposal
representative request/response for the new or changed endpoint
current vs proposed structure, config, or code shape

runbooks.name: demo_launch
runbooks.description: Demo or launch stage layout — feature carousel, before/after video pair, headline metric, reliability timeline.

sequence.data_hint: one slide per headline feature with screenshot and caption
left: previous behaviour; right: new behaviour
label: the launch KPI; value: current; previous: pre-launch baseline
service health blocks over the launch window

runbooks.name: learning_hub
runbooks.description: Learning Hub — a curriculum/revision study app: subject tabs across the top, slide pills per subject, each slide one learning interaction (timeline, drill, flashcards, quiz, takeaways, or method steps). First runbook-as-data instance (spec/runbook-pattern-v0.1.md in a2ui-private): the composition block below is the authored bill of materials; consumers shape content to the input_contract (see parsing_guide) and stamp via the MCP emit_runbook_surface tool. Judgment lives here, spent once — callers supply content only and cannot compose badly.

input_contract.title: string (required) — app title
input_contract.subjects: array (required) of {label, color?, slides}. label may carry an emoji prefix ("📐 Maths"); color is the subject's hex accent.
input_contract.slides: each slide = {label, kind, hero?, ...content fields by kind}. label is the pill text (short, emoji prefix welcome). hero (optional) = {heading, subtext?, badge?} — rendered as a banner above the slide content.

content_by_kind.drill: questions: array of {question, answer}; duration?: seconds (default 1200)
content_by_kind.flashcards: cards: array of {front, back}; label_front?; label_back?; accent?
content_by_kind.quiz: question: string; options: array of strings; correct: 0-based index; explanation: string

runbooks.parsing_guide:
Consumers shape their source into the input_contract; parsing is the consumer's documented job, composition is the runbook's. From a knowledge-catalogue curriculum.md (typed-section grammar, knowledge-catalogue/SPEC.md): {#timeline} sections -> kind timeline ("### YYYY | Title" lines become events); {#drill} -> kind drill; {#concept}/{#glossary} -> kind flashcards; [!PIÈGE] callouts -> kind quiz; {#key_takeaways} -> kind takeaways; {#method} -> kind method. One subject per curriculum domain, slide labels short. All content field shapes are renderer-ground-truth (extracted from the live brevet app's payload).
Pagination: the per-page size ceiling (~8100 chars) is enforced per subject, not per whole hub. If all subjects fit one page, the engine returns a single hub-as-one-page url (subject tabs + slide pills, all navigation client-side). If not, it automatically splits by subject, seeds each subject's own hub-as-one-page url server-side, and returns one hub-of-pages nav page linking them by short ?nav= reference (name overhead only, no embedded content — so total curriculum size has no real ceiling). Response carries `pages[]` + `seeded: true` when this happened. No consumer action needed either way; the returned url is ready to use as-is. See spec/nav-budget-pagination-v0.1.md (a2ui-private) for the design.

runbooks.name: sprint_review
runbooks.description: Sprint review stage layout — velocity and bug KPIs, completion progress, backlog table, burn-down chart.

sequence.data_hint: value: story points completed this sprint; delta: vs previous sprint; is_up from delta sign
value: open bug count; delta: change over sprint; accent red when rising
value: percent of committed scope done; label: sprint name; caption: days remaining
rows: carry-over and top backlog items with owner and status columns
labels: sprint days; datasets: [ideal burn-down, actual remaining points]

runbooks.name: team_standup
runbooks.description: Team standup stage layout — one bio card per person, blockers checklist, status badges.

sequence.data_hint: name, role, and yesterday/today summary per team member
title: Blockers; items: open blockers with owner in each string
one badge per workstream; color green/yellow/red by health; pulse on items needing attention
